simple_types.name: Compression_Level
simple_types.code: type Compression_Level is new Integer range -1 .. 9;

simple_types.name: Header_Type
simple_types.code: type Header_Type is (None, Auto, Default, GZip);

documentation.description:
Header type usage have a some limitation for inflate.
See comment for Inflate_Init.

simple_types.name: Memory_Level_Type
simple_types.code: type Memory_Level_Type is new Integer range 1 .. 9;

simple_types.name: Window_Bits_Type
simple_types.code: type Window_Bits_Type is new Integer range 8 .. 15;

tagged_types.name: Filter_Type
tagged_types.code: type Filter_Type is tagged limited private;

documentation.description:
The filter is for compression and for decompression.
The usage of the type is depend of its initialization.

Z_BLOCK requests that inflate() stop
if and when it get to the next deflate block boundary. When decoding the
zlib or gzip format, this will cause inflate() to return immediately
after the header and before the first block. When doing a raw inflate,
inflate() will go ahead and process the first block, and will return
when it gets to the end of that block, or when it runs out of data.
Only one method allowed in this ZLib version
Just for tell the compressor that input data is complete.

All output is flushed as with SYNC_FLUSH, and the compression state
is reset so that decompression can restart from this point if previous
compressed data has been damaged or if random access is desired. Using
Full_Flush too often can seriously degrade the compression.
Regular way for compression, no flush
Will be removed, use Z_SYNC_FLUSH instead

All pending output is flushed to the output buffer and the output
is aligned on a byte boundary, so that the decompressor can get all
input data available so far. (In particular avail_in is zero after the
call if enough output space has been provided  before the call.)
Flushing may degrade compression for some compression algorithms and so
it should be used only when necessary.

subprograms.name: Close

documentation.description:
Closing the compression or decompressor.
If stream is closing before the complete and Ignore_Error is False,
The exception would be raised.

subprograms.name: CRC32
subprograms.code:
function CRC32
  (CRC    : in Unsigned_32;
   Data   : in Ada.Streams.Stream_Element_Array)
   return Unsigned_32

documentation.description: Compute CRC32, it could be necessary for make gzip format

subprograms.name: CRC32

documentation.description: Compute CRC32, it could be necessary for make gzip format

subprograms.name: Deflate_Init

documentation.description:
Compressor initialization.
When Header parameter is Auto or Default, then default zlib header
would be provided for compressed data.
When Header is GZip, then gzip header would be set instead of
default header.
When Header is None, no header would be set for compressed data.

subprograms.name: Flush

documentation.description: Flushing the data from the compressor.

subprograms.name: Generic_Translate

documentation.description:
Compress/decompress data fetch from Data_In routine and pass the result
to the Data_Out routine. User should provide Data_In and Data_Out
for compression/decompression data flow.
Compression or decompression depend on Filter initialization.

subprograms.name: Inflate_Init

documentation.description:
Decompressor initialization.
Default header type mean that ZLib default header is expecting in the
input compressed stream.
Header type None mean that no header is expecting in the input stream.
GZip header type mean that GZip header is expecting in the
input compressed stream.
Auto header type mean that header type (GZip or Native) would be
detected automatically in the input stream.
Note that header types parameter values None, GZip and Auto are
supported for inflate routine only in ZLib versions 1.2.0.2 and later.
Deflate_Init is supporting all header types.

subprograms.name: Is_Open
subprograms.code: function Is_Open (Filter : in Filter_Type) return Boolean

documentation.description: Is the filter opened for compression or decompression.

subprograms.name: Read

documentation.description:
Compress/Decompress data from generic parameter procedure Read to the
Item. User should provide Buffer and initialized Rest_First, Rest_Last
indicators. If Allow_Read_Some is True, Read routines could return
Last < Item'Last only at end of stream.

subprograms.name: Stream_End
subprograms.code: function Stream_End (Filter : in Filter_Type) return Boolean

documentation.description: Return the true when the stream is complete.

subprograms.name: Total_In
subprograms.code: function Total_In (Filter : in Filter_Type) return Count

documentation.description: Returns total number of input bytes read so far

subprograms.name: Total_Out
subprograms.code: function Total_Out (Filter : in Filter_Type) return Count

documentation.description: Returns total number of bytes output so far

subprograms.name: Translate

documentation.description:
Compress/decompress the In_Data buffer and place the result into
Out_Data. In_Last is the index of last element from In_Data accepted by
the Filter. Out_Last is the last element of the received data from
Filter. To tell the filter that incoming data are complete put the
Flush parameter to Finish.
Return string representation of the ZLib version.

subprograms.name: Write

documentation.description:
Compress/Decompress data from Item to the generic parameter procedure
Write. Output buffer size could be set in Buffer_Size generic parameter.
